what_it_does: It is an exhaustive, interpretive inventory of the build-prep/ directory: it lists every sub-area, key documents, their stated purpose, status, decisions, stale or gap flags, nested directories, cross-area contracts, and a pointer table for loop builders.
observations: Documents 461 files across 27 top-level subdirectories and 21 nested sub-directories in build-prep/.
Provides a directory tree and per-sub-area sections with 'What it is', 'Key docs', 'Status', 'Key decisions/contracts', 'Stale/Gap flags', and 'Nested sub-dirs'.
Includes three root-file summaries: MODE-SYSTEM-MAP.md, CROSS-SESSION-CHANNELS-PROVEN.md, and cross-session-unified-transport.md.
Highlights the-one-application/ as the area with the most files and current loop-state, with MORNING-STACK.md and COMMISSION-COMPLETION-REGISTER.md as operational anchors.
Records specific anomalies: 2,793 unreachable vectors, channel:// and cluster:// missing from the SCHEMES tuple, and a name collision between projection.py and projections.py/projections/.
Contains a 'CROSS-AREA CONNECTIONS + CONTRACTS' section mapping the resolver, keystone gate, gated floor, four root axes, and loop-prep triad hierarchy.
Ends with a 'KEY ANCHORS / DECISION POINTERS' table mapping topics to file locations.
Self-describes as generated by a read-only dragnet sweep on 2026-06-21 and notes count reconciliation between sweeps.
standouts: It is a single-file map of the entire build-prep planning substrate, mixing inventory, status, and interpretation.
It explicitly flags stale, superseded, contradictory, incomplete, and surprising items rather than only listing files.
It identifies the-one-application/ as the current active loop-state home and the resolver as the cross-area spine.
It records concrete unresolved gaps such as the channel:// and cluster:// address-grammar gap and 2,793 unreachable backup vectors.
It includes an anchor-pointer table intended to help builders find topic-specific docs.
conventions: Uses a consistent per-area template: heading, 'What it is', 'Key docs', 'Status', 'Key decisions/contracts', 'Stale/Gap flags', 'Nested sub-dirs'.
Marks high-signal items with a star (★) and status with emoji-style indicators (🔴/🟡/✅).
Includes file sizes and dates for root files.
Cross-references other files using relative paths and bracketed memory links like [[feedback-no-episodic-memory]].
Breaks the usual brevity of an inventory by adding evaluative status labels, build-state judgments, and 'verify' notes.
concerns: The file is very long and manually maintained, so status labels will drift quickly as builds progress.
It mixes factual inventory with interpretive judgments (stale/superseded/built) without always citing the evidence inside the file.
Many entries end with 'verify' or 'pending' notes, indicating the inventory itself is not a source of ground truth for build state.
The file count and nested-directory counts are tied to a specific sweep date and may already be stale.
It self-acknowledges that the area inventory home was empty before this sweep, raising questions about how it will be kept in sync.
notes: A sprawling but structured map of all build-prep planning areas, meant to be read as a navigation aid and stale-item radar rather than as code ground truth.
questions: Which of the two OAuth patch files in claude-design/supabase/ is actually deployed?
Has the concurrent-cognition worktree on branch concurrent-cognition been merged to main?
What is the exact current code state of items marked 'pending' or 'verify'?
How will this inventory be updated after the sweep date (2026-06-21)?
Are the 461-file count and 21 nested-directory count still accurate?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it claims to be an exhaustive area inventory and indeed lists all sub-areas, key files, statuses, and connections.
apparent_themes: Resolver/face unification as the central design story
Operator-cycle and build-prep as the project's intent substrate
intention_signals: It is trying to turn a large, distributed planning directory into a single navigable control panel so builders can locate authoritative docs, spot drift, and understand dependencies without re-sweeping the tree.
novelty: It is a meta-inventory that treats design documents themselves as a substrate to be audited, complete with drift flags, supersession chains, and an anchor-pointer table — less a README than a planning-territory ledger.
contribution: It contributes a top-level index and status map of the build-prep corpus, including per-area summaries, stale/gap annotations, cross-area connection pointers, and an anchor table that a fused system could use for navigation and drift detection.
summary_for_embedding: An exhaustive, interpretive inventory of the build-prep/ directory listing sub-areas, key documents, statuses, decisions, stale/gap flags, nested directories, cross-area contracts, and anchor pointers for loop builders.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single navigable map and stale-item radar for the project's planning and design substrate.
